top_signals.title: India Hikes Fuel Prices for the Second Time in a Week
top_signals.summary: India’s state fuel traders raised retail prices for the second time in less than a week, as they seek to cushion the blow to margins from soaring crude prices while the war in the Middle East drags on with no resolution in sight. Retail fuel prices in the country were hiked by the equivalent of $0.0093 per litre, Reuters reported. This is a third of last week’s price hike, which was the first in four years, highlighting the pain that the world’s third-largest crude oil importer is experiencing because of the developments in the…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oil prices fall as Trump postpones Iran strike, easing supply disruption fears - CNBC
top_signals.summary: Oil prices fall as Trump postpones Iran strike, easing supply disruption fears  CNBC
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Former FTX Executive Launches a ‘No Loss’ AI Trading Platform
top_signals.summary: At the European arm of failed crypto exchange FTX, Patrick Gruhn watched as traders repeatedly lost money on risky bets. Now, he’s launching a product where the savviest investors can turn a profit — even while putting zero capital at stake.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: ‘Obscene’ – HS2 on track to cost at least £102bn as minister slams ‘gold-plated folly’
top_signals.summary: HS2 is now poised to cost the taxpayer as much as £102bn and will not deliver its first service from Euston until at least 2040, according to the transport secretary who blasted the project as a “massively over-specced folly”. Heidi Alexander said the cost of the ill-fated train line was now on course to nearly [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Monzo boss pledges European expansion as profits jump
top_signals.summary: Diana Layfield pointed to ‘Monzo-shaped hole’ in markets across Europe

top_signals.title: Everest Announces Agreement to Sell Colombia Insurance Operations to AIG
top_signals.summary: Everest Group, Ltd. (“Everest” or “the Company”) (NYSE: EG), a global specialty reinsurance and insurance leader, today announced a definitive agreement to sell Everest Compañía de Seguros Generales Colombia S.A. to American International Group, Inc. (NYSE: AIG). The transaction builds on Everest’s previously announced sale of its global Commercial Retail Insurance renewal rights to AIG [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Japan antitrust watchdog raids firms in Hokkaido Shinkansen bid-rigging probe
top_signals.summary: The probes targeted nine construction companies and a government-linked railway infrastructure agency.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: CreditNature and Stabiliti Partner to Embed Nature Finance into Everyday Economic Activity
top_signals.summary: CreditNature, a leading international Nature Fintech company, is proud to announce a strategic partnership with Stabiliti, which enables businesses to seamlessly integrate verified nature restoration into core economic activity and financial flows, unlocking scalable investment for ecosystem recovery.
